title: buxus sempervirens f. handsworthiensis
intro: Buxus sempervirens f. handsworthiensis, also known as the Boxwood or Common Box, is a compact, slow-growing evergreen shrub that is often used in topiary and hedges due to its small, rounded leaves. Traditionally, the leaves of this plant have been used in herbal medicine, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where they are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Buxus sempervirens f. handsworthiensis include reducing fever, alleviating rheumatism, and acting as a natural antiseptic to aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of Buxus sempervirens f. handsworthiensis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, although it's essential to consult a medical professional before using any herbal remedies.
